description.p: ZenNotes is a keyboard-first Markdown notes app that keeps your notes as ordinary Markdown files on disk — yours to own, sync, and version however you like.
On top of the files you already own, it adds:

ul.li: Vim-friendly editing with split and live-preview workflows
Wiki-links, tags, tasks, daily notes, and full-text search
Diagrams (Mermaid, TikZ), math (KaTeX), and syntax-highlighted code
CSV databases with Notion-style Table and Board views over plain .csv files
Archive, trash, quick capture, and a built-in command-line companion

screenshot.caption: Editing Markdown with live preview, sidebar, and tabs

description.p: A windowed sidebar that stays fast on huge vaults, a calmer redesigned Settings page, a new home view with your recent notes and today's tasks, Obsidian Excalidraw import, and image-rendering fixes in PDF export and Live Preview.
Link straight to your CSV databases with wikilinks, Notion-style slash commands in Quick Capture, and editor fixes (per-note undo, wikilinks in code, document outline).
A portable plain-text config file you can sync across machines, manual task reordering in notes and on the Tasks page, faster vault reopening, and remappable table navigation.
Real Markdown highlighting, system-wide quick capture on Wayland, manual note ordering, and the Kanagawa theme.
Maintenance release with editor and vault improvements.
Maintenance release with editor and vault improvements.
